sections.paragraph:
an unused root. The primary power appears to be that of pressing together, whether by treading or in any other way; comp. Arab. أَثَّثَ to tread, to trample on, to subdue (kindred to אֲצַץ to urge, and even אוּץ and the words there compared). Hence אֲשִׁישָׁה a cake made of dried figs pressed together; אָשִׁישׁ a foundation which is pressed down by treading on it, and Arab. أَسَّ Conj. II, to found, to make firm in laying a foundation, comp. אָשָׁה to prop.

Note.-הִתְאֹושֵׁשׁ see under the root אִישׁ , p. 41, A .
